claim: Anvil is a log-structured merge-tree storage engine designed for workloads dominated by sequential writes rather than random updates.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil is a log-structured merge-tree storage engine designed for workloads dominated by sequential writes rather than random updates.

claim: Once the memtable reaches a configured size threshold, Anvil flushes it to disk as an immutable sorted file, sometimes called a sorted string table or SSTable.

supporting_evidence.quote: Once the memtable reaches a configured size threshold, Anvil flushes it to disk as an immutable sorted file, sometimes called a sorted string table or SSTable.

claim: Anvil additionally writes every incoming operation to a write-ahead log before acknowledging it, so an in-memory-only memtable can be reconstructed after a crash.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil additionally writes every incoming operation to a write-ahead log before acknowledging it, so an in-memory-only memtable can be reconstructed after a crash.

claim: Anvil's default configuration flushes a memtable once it reaches 64 megabytes, a value chosen to balance flush frequency against per-flush overhead.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil's default configuration flushes a memtable once it reaches 64 megabytes, a value chosen to balance flush frequency against per-flush overhead.

claim: Anvil supports two compaction strategies: a tiered strategy that merges same-size files together, and a leveled strategy that organizes SSTables into size-based levels with bounded overlap.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil supports two compaction strategies: a tiered strategy that merges same-size files together, and a leveled strategy that organizes SSTables into size-based levels with bounded overlap.

claim: Leveled compaction favors read performance, since each level guarantees non-overlapping key ranges, but pays for this with substantially higher write amplification from more frequent merging.

supporting_evidence.quote: Leveled compaction favors read performance, since each level guarantees non-overlapping key ranges, but pays for this with substantially higher write amplification from more frequent merging.

claim: A Bloom filter can produce false positives, incorrectly suggesting a key might be present, but never false negatives, so a read never skips a file that actually contains its target key.

supporting_evidence.quote: A Bloom filter can produce false positives, incorrectly suggesting a key might be present, but never false negatives, so a read never skips a file that actually contains its target key.

claim: Anvil sizes each Bloom filter to target a false-positive rate of roughly one percent by default, trading additional memory for fewer wasted disk reads.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil sizes each Bloom filter to target a false-positive rate of roughly one percent by default, trading additional memory for fewer wasted disk reads.

claim: Write amplification measures how many bytes are actually written to disk for each byte of application data, and it is one of the central tradeoffs in any LSM design.

supporting_evidence.quote: Write amplification measures how many bytes are actually written to disk for each byte of application data, and it is one of the central tradeoffs in any LSM design.

claim: Anvil's leveled compaction under default settings produces a measured write amplification factor of roughly ten, meaning each byte of application data results in about ten bytes of total disk writes over its lifetime.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil's leveled compaction under default settings produces a measured write amplification factor of roughly ten, meaning each byte of application data results in about ten bytes of total disk writes over its lifetime.

claim: Range reads are more expensive, since they cannot rely on Bloom filters at all and must merge overlapping key ranges from every relevant SSTable using a heap-based merge iterator.

supporting_evidence.quote: Range reads are more expensive, since they cannot rely on Bloom filters at all and must merge overlapping key ranges from every relevant SSTable using a heap-based merge iterator.

claim: Leveled compaction's non-overlapping key ranges within a level substantially reduce the number of files a range read must merge, compared to tiered compaction's looser overlap guarantees.

supporting_evidence.quote: Leveled compaction's non-overlapping key ranges within a level substantially reduce the number of files a range read must merge, compared to tiered compaction's looser overlap guarantees.

claim: Anvil ships with three named presets, write-heavy, balanced, and read-heavy, that bundle sensible defaults for all three parameters together rather than requiring operators to tune each independently.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil ships with three named presets, write-heavy, balanced, and read-heavy, that bundle sensible defaults for all three parameters together rather than requiring operators to tune each independently.

claim: Most production deployments start from one of these presets and adjust only a single parameter afterward, rather than tuning from Anvil's raw defaults directly.

supporting_evidence.quote: Most production deployments start from one of these presets and adjust only a single parameter afterward, rather than tuning from Anvil's raw defaults directly.

claim: Anvil 1.0 released in March 2019 supported only tiered compaction, with leveled compaction added later as an alternative strategy.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil 1.0 released in March 2019 supported only tiered compaction, with leveled compaction added later as an alternative strategy.

claim: Anvil 1.4, released in January 2020, added leveled compaction as a selectable strategy alongside the original tiered approach.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil 1.4, released in January 2020, added leveled compaction as a selectable strategy alongside the original tiered approach.

claim: Anvil 2.0, shipped in August 2021, introduced configurable Bloom filter false-positive targets, replacing a previously fixed two-percent rate.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil 2.0, shipped in August 2021, introduced configurable Bloom filter false-positive targets, replacing a previously fixed two-percent rate.

claim: Several research groups have explored offloading compaction's merge-sort step to GPU hardware, evaluating results against the Aurora LSM Compaction Benchmark, known as ALCB.

supporting_evidence.quote: Several research groups have explored offloading compaction's merge-sort step to GPU hardware, evaluating results against the Aurora LSM Compaction Benchmark, known as ALCB.

claim: The ALCB reference dataset used in these studies totals 2.1 terabytes of synthetic key-value data, modeled on a mix of write-heavy and read-heavy access patterns.

supporting_evidence.quote: The ALCB reference dataset used in these studies totals 2.1 terabytes of synthetic key-value data, modeled on a mix of write-heavy and read-heavy access patterns.

claim: One published evaluation using Anvil's compaction engine against the ALCB dataset reported a 3.4x compaction throughput improvement when merge-sort was offloaded to GPU hardware.

supporting_evidence.quote: One published evaluation using Anvil's compaction engine against the ALCB dataset reported a 3.4x compaction throughput improvement when merge-sort was offloaded to GPU hardware.

claim: The Tessera GPU scheduler is a widely used research scheduler for coordinating many concurrent GPU kernels across shared hardware, and it was the scheduler used in the ALCB compaction offloading study.

supporting_evidence.quote: The Tessera GPU scheduler is a widely used research scheduler for coordinating many concurrent GPU kernels across shared hardware, and it was the scheduler used in the ALCB compaction offloading study.

claim: Tessera, as configured for that study, supported a maximum of 96 concurrent kernels per GPU device before additional kernels were queued rather than dispatched immediately.

supporting_evidence.quote: Tessera, as configured for that study, supported a maximum of 96 concurrent kernels per GPU device before additional kernels were queued rather than dispatched immediately.

claim: That concurrency ceiling meant the compaction study's throughput results reflect Tessera's specific scheduling limits rather than the theoretical maximum parallelism the underlying GPU hardware could otherwise support.

supporting_evidence.quote: That concurrency ceiling meant the compaction study's throughput results reflect Tessera's specific scheduling limits rather than the theoretical maximum parallelism the underlying GPU hardware could otherwise support.

claim: The tradeoffs between Anvil's two compaction strategies are summarized in the following comparison.

supporting_evidence.quote: The tradeoffs between Anvil's two compaction strategies are summarized in the following comparison.

claim: Space amplification measures how much disk space the engine uses beyond the logical size of the stored data, and leveled compaction's tighter bound comes directly from its more aggressive merging.

supporting_evidence.quote: Space amplification measures how much disk space the engine uses beyond the logical size of the stored data, and leveled compaction's tighter bound comes directly from its more aggressive merging.

claim: Bloom filter memory cost grows as the target false-positive rate shrinks, following the standard Bloom filter bits-per-key relationship.

supporting_evidence.quote: Bloom filter memory cost grows as the target false-positive rate shrinks, following the standard Bloom filter bits-per-key relationship.

claim: Anvil's one-percent default sits near the point of diminishing returns, where further reductions in false-positive rate cost meaningfully more memory per additional percentage point avoided.

supporting_evidence.quote: Anvil's one-percent default sits near the point of diminishing returns, where further reductions in false-positive rate cost meaningfully more memory per additional percentage point avoided.

claim: Operators seeing degraded read latency should work through the following checklist before escalating to a full reconfiguration.

supporting_evidence.quote: Operators seeing degraded read latency should work through the following checklist before escalating to a full reconfiguration.

claim: The first item, compaction falling behind, is by far the most common root cause reported across Anvil deployments, according to the project's public incident archive.

supporting_evidence.quote: The first item, compaction falling behind, is by far the most common root cause reported across Anvil deployments, according to the project's public incident archive.

claim: New Anvil deployments should complete the following steps before being exposed to production traffic.

supporting_evidence.quote: New Anvil deployments should complete the following steps before being exposed to production traffic.

claim: Skipping the reference benchmark step is the most common cause of unpleasant surprises during a cluster's first real traffic spike.

supporting_evidence.quote: Skipping the reference benchmark step is the most common cause of unpleasant surprises during a cluster's first real traffic spike.

claim: Matsumoto and Endo's ログ構造化マージツリーにおける書き込み増幅の解析 (2015) provides one of the earliest detailed write-amplification analyses that later leveled-compaction designs, including Anvil's, cite directly.

supporting_evidence.quote: Matsumoto and Endo's ログ構造化マージツリーにおける書き込み増幅の解析 (2015) provides one of the earliest detailed write-amplification analyses that later leveled-compaction designs, including Anvil's, cite directly.

claim: Volkova's Оптимизация компактации в LSM-деревьях (2018) proposes a hybrid compaction scheduling heuristic that influenced Anvil's own compaction scheduler.

supporting_evidence.quote: Volkova's Оптимизация компактации в LSM-деревьях (2018) proposes a hybrid compaction scheduling heuristic that influenced Anvil's own compaction scheduler.

claim: For a system with L levels and a size ratio T between adjacent levels, the standard bound on write amplification is approximately L * T, since each level's data is rewritten roughly T times before moving to the next level.

supporting_evidence.quote: For a system with L levels and a size ratio T between adjacent levels, the standard bound on write amplification is approximately L * T, since each level's data is rewritten roughly T times before moving to the next level.

claim: The measured value of roughly 10 reported in the write amplification section is well below this worst-case bound, reflecting that not every level reaches its maximum size simultaneously in a typical workload.

supporting_evidence.quote: The measured value of roughly 10 reported in the write amplification section is well below this worst-case bound, reflecting that not every level reaches its maximum size simultaneously in a typical workload.

claim: Pressure changed once a second internal team adopted Anvil for a read-heavy lookup service, and quickly found tiered compaction's read amplification unacceptable for their latency requirements.

supporting_evidence.quote: Pressure changed once a second internal team adopted Anvil for a read-heavy lookup service, and quickly found tiered compaction's read amplification unacceptable for their latency requirements.

claim: An early leveled-compaction implementation had a subtle bug where a burst of writes could temporarily leave a level far above its target size, triggering a cascading series of merges that briefly starved foreground reads of I/O bandwidth.

supporting_evidence.quote: An early leveled-compaction implementation had a subtle bug where a burst of writes could temporarily leave a level far above its target size, triggering a cascading series of merges that briefly starved foreground reads of I/O bandwidth.

claim: Looking back, the team identifies the internal read-heavy team's original complaint as the single event that most shaped Anvil's subsequent design, since nearly every major feature added since, leveled compaction, the compaction rate limiter, and configurable Bloom filters, traces back to that same underlying tension between write and read performance.

supporting_evidence.quote: Looking back, the team identifies the internal read-heavy team's original complaint as the single event that most shaped Anvil's subsequent design, since nearly every major feature added since, leveled compaction, the compaction rate limiter, and configurable Bloom filters, traces back to that same underlying tension between write and read performance.

claim: SSTable count on the affected nodes grew from a typical steady-state count of around 40 files to over 300 files within six hours, each additional file adding a small but cumulative cost to every point read.

supporting_evidence.quote: SSTable count on the affected nodes grew from a typical steady-state count of around 40 files to over 300 files within six hours, each additional file adding a small but cumulative cost to every point read.

claim: The immediate operational response was to manually throttle incoming writes at the application layer, giving compaction a chance to catch up without the write rate continuing to outpace it.

supporting_evidence.quote: The immediate operational response was to manually throttle incoming writes at the application layer, giving compaction a chance to catch up without the write rate continuing to outpace it.

claim: No data was lost during the incident, since the write-ahead log continued accepting and durably recording writes throughout; the backlog affected only compaction and downstream read latency, never write durability.

supporting_evidence.quote: No data was lost during the incident, since the write-ahead log continued accepting and durably recording writes throughout; the backlog affected only compaction and downstream read latency, never write durability.

claim: This recap restates the memtable flush trigger for readers skimming only the overview section.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap restates the memtable flush trigger for readers skimming only the overview section.

claim: The exact threshold value is configurable per deployment and is discussed in full in the memtable section rather than repeated here.

supporting_evidence.quote: The exact threshold value is configurable per deployment and is discussed in full in the memtable section rather than repeated here.

claim: This recap restates the ALCB dataset size for readers who only skim the GPU-acceleration section of this document.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap restates the ALCB dataset size for readers who only skim the GPU-acceleration section of this document.

claim: The dataset composition, a mix of write-heavy and read-heavy access patterns, has remained unchanged across every published study that references it.

supporting_evidence.quote: The dataset composition, a mix of write-heavy and read-heavy access patterns, has remained unchanged across every published study that references it.
